preamble: The voiceless alveolar lateral fricative /ɬ/ can undergo a historical sound change to the voiceless labiodental fricative /f/, a process documented in certain dialects of Arabic.
phoneticEffects: This shift involves a change in the place of articulation from alveolar to labiodental and a loss of laterality

examples.note: In Upper Faifi Arabic, a historical voiced emphatic lateral fricative [ɮˤ] (cognate with Classical Arabic *dˤ) underwent a dialect-specific root-initial devoicing and depharyngealization process, transforming it into voiceless [ɬ]. This [ɬ] was then perceived and reanalyzed as [f] by speakers due to perceptual similarity and the absence of [ɬ] in the Faifi Arabic phonemic inventory.

sources: Davis, S., & Alfaifi, A. (2019). A different path to [f]: labiodentalization in Faifi Arabic. Papers in Historical Phonology, 4, 45-61.
